title: Sudan Conflict Update — Mar 26, 2026
summary: Sudan's civil war escalated dramatically in mid-to-late March 2026 with a devastating surge in drone warfare killing over 500 civilians since January 1, and more than 200 in just the two weeks following March 4. On Eid al-Fitr (March 20), air and drone strikes struck El Daein Teaching Hospital in East Darfur, killing at least 64 people including 13 children — pushing the total killed in healthcare attacks above 2,000 since the war began. On the battlefield, the RSF recaptured the strategic city of Bara in North Kordofan on March 16 and simultaneously seized the border town of Karnoi, continuing its strategy to encircle SAF headquarters at El Obeid. The conflict spilled further into Chad when an RSF drone killed 17 people in the border town of Tine on March 18, prompting President Déby to close Sudan's 1,300 km border and threaten military retaliation.

title: Sudan Conflict Update — Mar 22, 2026
summary: Sudan's catastrophic civil war enters day 1072 with two formally declared famines in El-Fasher and Kadugli, and 400,000 people facing starvation. OHCHR published findings in February 2026 concluding RSF violations during the capture of El Fasher constitute war crimes. Fighting intensified in Kordofan in early 2026 with almost daily drone strikes on civilian infrastructure including markets and health facilities. The UN Fact-Finding Mission previously concluded the RSF committed genocide against non-Arab Indigenous groups. Sudan hosts the world's largest humanitarian crisis with 24.6 million food insecure and 12.1 million displaced.
